repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Scrapbooks of John M. Ferris III, Cornell University class of 1951. Contains clippings, ephemera, correspondence, and photographs relating to his time at Cornell.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John M. Ferris III graduated with a BS from Cornell University in 1951 and a PhD in 1956.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John M. Ferris III scrapbooks, #37-5-4493. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Scrapbooks
Cornell University--Pictorial works
controlaccess.corpname: New York State College of Agriculture. Farm and Home Week
New York State College of Agriculture

did.physdesc: The spine of this scrapbook is partially dismembered and the pages inside are no longer bound.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The box contains an Alpha Tau Omega fraternity 1951 class picture and Ivy football tickets. Included in the scrapbook are news clippings, photographs, and napkins from the wedding of Ferris to his wife, Virginia J. Ferris (née Rogers) and memos from Purdue University, where both Ferris and his wife became professors of Entomology. Ferris included many photographs and ephemera from their travels across the Midwest, New York, and Canada; maps, travel guides, dried flowers, and hotel room stationery among them.

did.physdesc: This scrapbook is warped and overstuffed. Pages contain annotations and are prone to tearing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook contains photographs of John M. Ferris III, Alpha Tau Omega, Spring Weekend, football, the Big Red Band, the construction of Mann Library, The Dennis Club, housing floor plans, the Golden Nemotode Lab in Hicksville, NY, and even an aerial view of campus. Ephemera from the College of Agriculture, class activities (freshman through senior year), Freshman Club, Fall 1947 rush, The Cornell Dramatic Club, Willard Straight Hall activities, Christian Science organization, Spring Weekend, Farm and Home Week, concerts, Octagon musicals, and athletics. Also included are letters from Ferris to his family and memos from his future wife, Virgina J. Ferris.
